  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A material logical inconsistency is present: the artifact claims 87.4% AUROC is 'the strongest published result on this benchmark to date,' yet the only cited prior benchmark (Liu and Zhang, 2019) reports 91.2% AUROC at the same 50% coverage point — a higher number. This internal contradiction makes logical-consistency review central regardless of the delegation's vagueness.",
    "R1.3": "The key contribution claim (RadAbstain achieves state-of-the-art at 50% coverage) is directly undercut by a figure in the related-work paragraph. Evidence-claim alignment must be checked to determine whether the 87.4 vs. 91.2 discrepancy reflects a different metric, a different evaluation protocol, or an uncorrected overclaim.",
    "R1.4": "Novelty rests entirely on the comparative performance claim against Liu and Zhang (2019), described as the only prior benchmark. If that comparison is reversed, the delta-vs-prior-work narrative collapses, making novelty assessment load-bearing contingent on resolving R1.3.",
    "R1.6": "The delegation 'Help me with this section' is generically compatible with writing-polish requests; sentence-level and paragraph-level quality review is a plausible primary intent and cannot be ruled out without clarification."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want writing and style improvements only, or should I also assess the substantive claims, logic, and evidence alignment — including the apparent inconsistency between the reported 87.4% AUROC and the 91.2% figure from Liu and Zhang (2019) cited as the prior benchmark?"
}

rationale.R1.2: A material logical inconsistency is present: the artifact claims 87.4% AUROC is 'the strongest published result on this benchmark to date,' yet the only cited prior benchmark (Liu and Zhang, 2019) reports 91.2% AUROC at the same 50% coverage point — a higher number. This internal contradiction makes logical-consistency review central regardless of the delegation's vagueness.
rationale.R1.3: The key contribution claim (RadAbstain achieves state-of-the-art at 50% coverage) is directly undercut by a figure in the related-work paragraph. Evidence-claim alignment must be checked to determine whether the 87.4 vs. 91.2 discrepancy reflects a different metric, a different evaluation protocol, or an uncorrected overclaim.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty rests entirely on the comparative performance claim against Liu and Zhang (2019), described as the only prior benchmark. If that comparison is reversed, the delta-vs-prior-work narrative collapses, making novelty assessment load-bearing contingent on resolving R1.3.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'Help me with this section' is generically compatible with writing-polish requests; sentence-level and paragraph-level quality review is a plausible primary intent and cannot be ruled out without clarification.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want writing and style improvements only, or should I also assess the substantive claims, logic, and evidence alignment — including the apparent inconsistency between the reported 87.4% AUROC and the 91.2% figure from Liu and Zhang (2019) cited as the prior benchmark?
